input.background: The probability of a defective unit in a manufacturing process is q = 0.06. That means, the probability of a successful unit is p = 0.94. Using the normal approximation, calculate the probability that the number of defective units in a sample of 1000 units is between 50 and 70.
input.question: Using the normal approximation, calculate the probability that the number of defective units in a sample of 1000 units is between 50 and 70.

output.answer:
The probability that the number of defective units in a sample of 1000 units is between 50 and 70 is given by using the normal approximation:

$$
\begin{aligned}
P(50 \leq X \leq 70) & \simeq \Phi\left(\frac{70.5-60}{\sqrt{56.4}}\right)-\Phi\left(\frac{49.5-60}{\sqrt{56.4}}\right) \ \text { as } n q=60 \ \text { is large } \\
& =0.8379
\end{aligned}
$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
